proportion_7.label: Proportional Relationships
misconceptions: All linear relationships are proportional
Constant of proportionality can be negative in G7 contexts
Additive thinking instead of multiplicative
proportion_7.advanced: Represents proportional relationships algebraically (y = kx), graphically, and with tables. Determines and interprets the constant of proportionality in real-world contexts. Explains what points (x, y) on the graph mean, including (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.
proportion_7.proficient: Decides whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship using tables, graphs, or equations. Computes unit rates including those with complex fractions. Identifies the constant of proportionality (k = y/x).
proportion_7.developing: Identifies proportional relationships in tables when the ratio is simple. Finds unit rates with whole numbers. Recognizes that proportional graphs pass through the origin.
proportion_7.beginning: Recognizes that proportional means "constant ratio" but cannot consistently identify or compute k. Finds unit rates for simple whole-number ratios only.

percent_7.label: Percent Problems
misconceptions: A 50% increase followed by a 50% decrease returns to the original
Percent increase and percent of are the same computation
Sales tax is always added after discount
percent_7.advanced: Solves multi-step percent problems including successive percent changes, simple interest, and tax/tip/commission scenarios. Uses proportional reasoning to find the original amount given the result of a percent change.
percent_7.proficient: Solves problems involving percent increase, percent decrease, markups, markdowns, tax, tip, and simple interest. Uses proportional relationships (part/whole = percent/100) to find any unknown.
percent_7.developing: Calculates simple percent of a number. Solves percent increase or decrease with support. Can set up a proportion but may make errors solving.
percent_7.beginning: Finds 10% and 50% of a number. Understands percent means "per hundred" but cannot solve percent change problems without step-by-step guidance.

rational number_7.label: Operations with Rational Numbers
misconceptions: Negative × negative = negative
A negative divided by a negative is negative
rational number_7.advanced: Adds, subtracts, multiplies, and divides rational numbers fluently (including complex fractions). Solves real-world problems requiring all four operations with positive and negative rational numbers. Interprets sums, products, quotients, and differences of rational numbers in context.
rational number_7.proficient: Performs all four operations with integers and rational numbers (fractions, decimals). Understands p + q as the number located |q| units from p in the positive or negative direction. Converts between fraction and decimal forms of rational numbers.
rational number_7.developing: Adds and subtracts integers using a number line or rules. Multiplies integers. Struggles with division of negative fractions. Converts simple fractions to decimals.
rational number_7.beginning: Adds positive and negative integers with a number line. Multiplies positive integers fluently but is uncertain with negatives. Cannot yet operate with negative fractions/decimals.

equation_7.label: Equations & Inequalities
misconceptions: Inequality flips for all operations (not just multiplying/dividing by negative)
Terms must have the same variable AND exponent to combine
Distributing only to the first term inside parentheses
equation_7.advanced: Solves multi-step real-world and mathematical problems with positive and negative rational numbers. Writes and solves two-step equations and inequalities in the form px + q = r and px + q > r. Uses properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions and justify solution steps.
equation_7.proficient: Writes and solves two-step equations and inequalities. Represents solutions for inequalities on number lines. Applies properties of operations to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions.
equation_7.developing: Solves two-step equations with whole numbers. Writes equations from word problems with support. Graphs inequality solutions on a number line with direction prompts.
equation_7.beginning: Solves one-step equations. Recognizes that an inequality allows many solutions. Needs guided steps to set up or solve two-step problems.

scale_7.label: Scale Drawings & Similar Figures
misconceptions: Scale factor applies to area the same way as length
Similar figures are always the same orientation
A scale of 1:100 means the drawing is 100× bigger
scale_7.advanced: Reproduces a scale drawing at a different scale. Computes actual lengths and areas from scale drawings. Generalizes the critical attributes of similarity including ratios within and between similar shapes and applies to real-world design problems.
scale_7.proficient: Solves problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures, including computing actual lengths from the scale. Identifies corresponding sides and angles in similar figures. Uses ratios to determine if two shapes are similar.
scale_7.developing: Uses a given scale factor to find one missing length. Identifies similar figures when told they are similar. Struggles to set up proportions independently.
scale_7.beginning: Understands that a scale drawing is a proportional representation but cannot independently find actual measurements or determine similarity.

circle_7.label: Circles & Circumference
misconceptions: Diameter = 2 × circumference
Using diameter in the area formula instead of radius
π = 3.14 exactly (not an approximation)
circle_7.advanced: Solves real-world and mathematical problems involving area and circumference of circles and composite figures including semicircles and quarter circles. Explains the relationship between circumference and diameter (π) and derives the area formula using reasoning.
circle_7.proficient: Knows the formulas for circumference (C = πd or C = 2πr) and area (A = πr²) and uses them to solve problems. Describes π as the ratio of circumference to diameter. Uses 3.14 or 22/7 as approximations.
circle_7.developing: Finds circumference and area of circles when given the radius and reminded of the formulas. Confuses radius and diameter in some problems.
circle_7.beginning: Identifies radius, diameter, and circumference on a circle diagram. Cannot yet apply formulas independently to find circumference or area.

area_7.label: Area, Surface Area & Volume
misconceptions: Surface area = volume
Using the wrong height (slant vs. perpendicular)
All faces of a pyramid are triangles (base may be a rectangle)
area_7.advanced: Solves real-world problems involving area, surface area, and volume of 3-D figures including composite shapes. Describes cross-sections of 3-D figures. Computes lateral and total surface area of prisms and pyramids. Finds area of composite figures with semicircles.
area_7.proficient: Solves problems involving area of composite figures (rectangles, triangles, circles). Finds the volume and surface area of rectangular and triangular prisms and pyramids. Uses nets to compute surface area.
area_7.developing: Finds the area of basic figures and volume of rectangular prisms. Calculates surface area of rectangular prisms using nets with support. Struggles with pyramids and composite figures.
area_7.beginning: Finds the area of rectangles and triangles. Finds volume of rectangular prisms by counting cubes. Cannot yet compute surface area independently.

probability_7.label: Probability
misconceptions: Past outcomes affect future independent events (gambler's fallacy)
Probability must be expressed as a fraction only
All outcomes are equally likely
probability_7.advanced: Designs and uses simulations to generate frequencies for compound events. Compares experimental and theoretical probabilities, explaining sources of discrepancy. Finds probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and the counting principle.
probability_7.proficient: Finds theoretical and experimental probabilities of simple events. Represents sample spaces for compound events using tree diagrams and organized lists. Understands that probability is between 0 and 1 and identifies complementary events.
probability_7.developing: Finds theoretical probability of simple events. Creates sample spaces for simple compound events with guidance. Understands that probability of 0 is impossible and 1 is certain.
probability_7.beginning: Identifies outcomes as likely/unlikely. Finds probability of simple events (coin flip, die roll) with support. Cannot yet organize compound sample spaces.

statistics_7.label: Statistics & Sampling
misconceptions: Any sample represents the population
Larger samples are always biased-free
Mean is always the best measure of center
statistics_7.advanced: Uses data from a random sample to make valid inferences about a population. Assesses the validity of inferences by comparing samples. Informally assesses the degree of visual overlap of two numerical data distributions with similar variabilities and draws conclusions about population differences.
statistics_7.proficient: Understands that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a representative sample. Uses random samples to draw inferences. Compares two populations using measures of center and variability from random samples.
statistics_7.developing: Describes the difference between a sample and a population. Recognizes that random samples tend to be representative. Compares two data sets by looking at dot plots or box plots side by side.
statistics_7.beginning: Recognizes that data comes from a group but does not distinguish sample from population. Reads comparative displays with support but cannot draw independent conclusions about populations.
